div1.entryFree: Prison, vb. to confine, to restrain from liberty: a lily --ed in a gaol of snow, Ven. 362. her tears . . . being --ed in her eye like pearls in glass, Ven. 362 his true respect will p. false desire, Lucr. 642. p. my heart in thy steel bosom's ward, Sonn. 133, 9. universal plodding --s up the nimble spirits in the arteries, LLL IV, 3, 305 (O. Edd. poisons). the --ed bird, Cymb. III, 3, 43.
